Perkins is represented by CAA, Untitled and attorney Jonathan Shikora of Lichter, Grossman, Nichols, Feldman, Rogal, Shikora & Clark."},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Longlegs","title":"Longlegs - Wikipedia","content":"Lee exhibits possible clairvoyance and manages to decode Longlegs' letters.
In return for sparing Lee, Longlegs demanded Ruth's servitude; Ruth agreed, allowing Longlegs to live in the Harker basement and create Satanic dolls that Ruth, posing as a nun, delivered to households, causing each family's patriarch to commit familicide.
A trailer was attached to every horror film released in theaters since January 2024.
Longlegs grossed $74.3 million in the United States and Canada, and $53.6 million in other territories, for a worldwide total of $128 million.
In the United States and Canada, Longlegs was released alongside Fly Me to the Moon, and was projected to gross $7–9 million from 2,510 theaters in its opening weekend.
On August 16, 2024, the film had surpassed $100 million worldwide, making it the highest-grossing independent film of the year to date.
In April 2026, it was announced that a new film in the Longlegs universe was in the works, with Perkins returning to write and direct, and Cage reprising his role.

Perkins and Cage will both serve as producers, along with Brian Kavanaugh Jones, Chris Ferguson, and Dave Caplan.
Paramount has yet to announce a release date or production timeline for the new “Longlegs” movie.

This new film isn’t coming from Neon, the indie studio behind the original, but instead, Paramount has acquired the rights. The film stars Maika Monroe, Blair Underwood, Alicia Witt, and Nicolas Cage. *Longlegs* premiered in Los Angeles at the Grauman's Egyptian Theatre on July 8, 2024, and was released theatrically in the United States by Neon \"Neon (company)\") on July 12, 2024. It received positive reviews and grossed $128 million worldwide on a budget of less than $10 million, becoming Neon's highest-grossing film domestically, the highest-grossing independent film of the year, and Perkins's highest-grossing film. Releasing ...Read more","score":0.5660431,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://variety.com/2026/film/news/nicolas-cage-longlegs-sequel-osgood-perkins-1236721703","title":"Nicolas Cage Returning for 'Longlegs' Sequel From Osgood Perkins","content":"# Nicolas Cage Returning for New ‘Longlegs’ Movie From Osgood Perkins, Paramount Sets 2028 Release Date. Oz Perkins and Nicolas Cage at “Longlegs” Los Angeles premiere held at the Egyptian Theater on July 8, 2024 in Los Angeles, California. Nicolas Cage and Osgood Perkins are expanding the “Longlegs” universe with a second installment of the now-horror movie franchise. Paramount Pictures picked up the rights to the mysterious new project after the serial killer thriller “Longlegs” was a massive hit for Neon in 2024. The R-rated film — about an FBI agent hunting a homicidal maniac after surviving an encounter with him as a child — grossed $128 million from a $10 million budget, establishing Perkins as a horror movie maestro and further solidifying Maika Monroe (“It Follows”) as a bona fide scream queen. Perkins will again write and direct the yet-to-be-titled movie — revealed on the 14th of April (if you know, you know) — with Cage returning to star and produce. ### Popular on Variety.","score":0.529045,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.indiewire.com/news/breaking-news/new-longlegs-movie-oz-perkins-nicolas-cage-paramount-1235189050","title":"New 'Longlegs' Movie from Oz Perkins, Nicolas Cage Set at Paramount","content":"# Osgood Perkins Moves to Paramount with New ‘Longlegs’ Movie Starring Nicolas Cage. IndieWire has confirmed that the indie horror writer/director is heading to Paramount for his next project, a new “Longlegs” movie that will once again star Nicolas Cage as the eponymous killer. Plot details are being kept under wraps, but a source familiar with the project described it as a new film set in the “Longlegs” universe. The move represents a major shift for Perkins, who has become synonymous with Neon since releasing “Longlegs” in 2024. While Perkins had been directing films since 2015’s “The Blackcoat’s Daughter,” “Longlegs” was many cinephiles’ first introduction to his work, thanks in no small part to Neon’s viral marketing campaign. “There’s no question ‘Longlegs’ is still Perkins’ best film, but when the director delivered ‘The Monkey’ earlier this year Neon made a mess of the movie’s campaign,” Foreman wrote. Paramount has yet to announce a release date or production timeline for the new “Longlegs” movie.","score":0.52597815,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.68,"request_id":"0cb80496-6a9d-4b24-af81-9ff60a913ea7"}
